title: Error with IF formula
excerpt:
Hello. 
I have a table with 5 columns : A, B, C are columns with values, whereas D and E are formulas. 
D formula is 
{B}+{C} 
and E formula is  : 
IF(({A} = 0), 0, ({D}/{A})) 
What I dont understand is when A equals 0, …

posts.author: PierreM (PB)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello.

I have a table with 5 columns : A, B, C are columns with values, whereas D and E are formulas.
D formula is
{B}+{C}
and E formula is :
IF(({A} = 0), 0, ({D}/{A}))

What I dont understand is when A equals 0, the E column returns an error “Impossible to divide by 0”. Shouldn’t it print 0 ? Why is it still trying to do D / A ?

image706×203 2.52 KB

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@PierreM

The answer is in one of the monday formulas videos I did… Don’t remember which one.

The short answer is to use one of these:

IF(({A} = 0), 0, DIVIDE({D}, {A}))

OR

IF(({A} = 0), 0, ({D}/IF({A}=0, 1, {A})))

Jim - The Monday Man - YouTube Channel

posts.author: PierreM (PB)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the help @JCorrell , my problem is solved. This a bit strange to evaluate everything in the formula but at least I’m aware of this now.
I think you were talking about this video btw
